text:
It was a quiet morning. I was sitting quietly in my garden on my old rocking chair reading a book. A rabbit scampered by, its golden fur blending into the yellow flowers. The book had pictures of owls. I thought about owls all day. At that very moment, just outside my window, I spotted a shadow move ever so slightly. I looked and there was a fox! It wasn't very friendly.

It wasn't polite.

It trotted closer, its nose twitching ever so slightly... maybe it was curious about the book?

"No, fox!" I scolded. I didn't want it to read my book.

The fox just tilted its head. It might have felt my annoyance?

As it trotted back into the woods, I contemplated how animals behave. I decided maybe I envy foxes so much I want one. Of course, I wouldn't chase it down.

Instead, I've bought a couple of owls.

Now, every time I think
